title: Module 1
content:
Introduction to automation: classification of drives-hydraulic and
pneumatic-comparison. ISO symbol of fluid power generating elements-
hydraulic pumps and motor gears, vane pumps, piston pumps.
Control and regulating elements: Pressure, flow and direction control
valves, spool valves, electro hydraulic servo valves.

title: Module 2
content:
Electrical control of pneumatic and hydraulic circuits: interfacing with
PLC, ladder diagrams, use of relays, timers and counters.
Typical Design methods of automation-ladder diagram, circuit sequencing,
combinational logic design, cascading, Karnaugh map method.

title: Module 3
content:
Introduction to robotics: Drive systems: AC and DC servo and stepper
motors. Actuators: Pneumatic and hydraulic actuators, Sensors: Proximity
and range sensors, force and torque sensors, velocity sensors, encoders,
vision sensors.
Classification of robots: Work envelope, Classification of work cell layout.
Consideration in work cell design. Flexible automation vs robotic
technology.

title: Module 4
content:
Industrial application of robots: Spray painting, spot-welding, arc
welding, drilling, assembly operation, loading unloading, role of a robot in a
manufacturing cell. Safety considerations.
